metadata.description: Tools for turning scientific models into interactive, explorable GUIs.

plugins.name: model-scope
plugins.description: Hand it a paper and get a simulator: /model-scope:from-paper reads ONLY the reference, interviews you about the visualization purpose + specific requests, then builds the interactive simulation GUI and serves it at a verified local URL (with an optional shareable tunnel/permanent deploy when tooling allows). Or turn ANY parameterised model into an interactive explorer: move a slider and watch the simulation update, shown in views the MODEL defines (no fixed graphic/axis) — Bayesian/ideal-observer, efficient-coding & sequential observers, causal inference, working memory, decision (drift-diffusion + a model-comparison toggle), early vision (image input), LIF spiking neuron, reinforcement learning, an attractor network (energy landscape), a spatial SIR epidemic (macro), or any Monte-Carlo process — spanning the behavioural, single-neuron, sensory, network, and macro scales. Philosophy: replicate a model faithfully, illuminate it from the angles that fit its class, and let the user compare what qualitative and quantitative differences arise from the parameter and model choices — not a fixed pipeline, and a model with structure shows that structure first. A lens switch shows the class-appropriate angles (a decision model: one trial and many-trial statistics; a network: structure, activity, representation, energy landscape; an image/CNN model: architecture then layer transforms; an observer: measurement, likelihood, posterior, metrics). Idioms: a continuous playhead, a process-mode stage stepper, and the lens switch. Build a whole paper as a few screens (structure, condition comparisons, prediction), separating conditions from parameters; heavy screens stream via runChunks and a loading overlay. Schema-driven sliders and toggles, a simulate-to-views contract, a small plotting helper, no build step. Includes a modelbook of canonical model families (grounded in real open source), a reusable code library (mslib.js), and a GUI QC pipeline.
